text:
HexStrike AI MCP Agents Automating Cybersecurity with AI 

HexStrike AI MCP Agents is a powerful Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that links AI agents (Claude, GPT, Copilot, and more) with 150+ cybersecurity tools.

BloFin launches the BloFin MCP Server.

An open-source Model Context Protocol implementation connecting AI agents to trading infrastructure with 40 developer tools.

A step toward BloFin’s mission to build top-tier AI financial infrastructure.
Nyne, founded by a father-son duo, gives AI agents the human context they’re missing

Alibaba Cloud & Panasonic at AWE 2026: Jaime Xu, Alibaba Cloud Qwen model solution architect, showcased our Model Studio suite, empowering Panasonic’s new AI agents with multi-modal capabilities for personalized smart living. 

#AlibabaCloud #Panasonic #AWE2026 #SmartHome #Qwen

mcp (model context protocol) integration

this is openclaw's real power... you can connect to 1000+ vehicles

but the same rule applies here: don't say "open everything"

mcp's I recommend for starting:
- file system (start with read-only)
- web browsing
- calendar integration

AI agents = Model + Harness. The harness gives memory, tools, safe execution, context management, and long-term planning, turning raw intelligence into useful work. Even as models improve, harnesses remain key for real-world autonomy.

I built an MCP server for FlexRadio SmartSDR.

Now AI agents can control a ham radio.

Tune frequency  
Read signal meters  
Change RF power  
Switch antennas  

This opens the door to AI-assisted DX hunting, autonomous listeners, and agent-operated stations.

Repo:

The MCP is the standard that allows connecting Claude to any external system — Notion, GitHub, database — without custom code.

Understand the architecture and see how to start using it today.


https://
techknow.com.br/post/o-que-e-m
cp-model-context-protocol
…

#tech #ia #mcp

What it implements:

→ CAVLC + CABAC entropy decoding (460 context models)
→ 4x4 and 8x8 inverse transforms
→ 27 intra prediction modes
→ Quarter-pixel motion comp for P and B frames
→ MP4 demuxing, deblocking filter, reference picture management
Here's the context:  Google and Microsoft jointly released WebMCP, the Web Model Context Protocol, in February 2026 as a W3C standard proposal.  The core idea is simple but profound:
